item.title: Valladolid 7s: Australia to meet the USA in women's cup final
item.description: Australia will meet the USA in the final of the women's division at the Valladolid 7s in Spain at 1.46am tomorrow. Australia edged New Zealand 28-26 in the first semi-final while the USA beat C anada 14-12 in the second semi-final.
item.content:encoded:
Australia will meet the USA in the final of the women's division at the Valladolid 7s in Spain at 1.46am tomorrow.

Australia edged New Zealand 28-26 in the first semi-final while the USA beat C anada 14-12 in the second semi-final.

Looking at other matches, Argentina will face Great Britain at 9.31pm in the 11th place playoff, South Africa will meet Brazil at 10.15pm in the 9th place playoff, the Fijiana 7s will take on Spain at 11.04pm in the 7th place playoff, France will meet Japan at 11.58pm in the 5th place playoff, and New Zealand will face Canada at in the 3rd place playoff.

item.title: Fiji U18 women's handball team set for historic World Championship debut
item.description: Fiji's Under-18 women's handball team is set to make history as it prepares to compete at the International Handball Federation (IHF) Women's Youth World Championship in Romania. The team's qualification is a landmark achievement, with Fiji becoming the first Pacific Island nation to take part in the tournament.
item.content:encoded:
Fiji's Under-18 women's handball team is set to make history as it prepares to compete at the International Handball Federation (IHF) Women's Youth World Championship in Romania.

The team's qualification is a landmark achievement, with Fiji becoming the first Pacific Island nation to take part in the tournament.

Those selected for the final squad will carry the hopes of not only Fiji but the entire Pacific region when they take on the world's best young handball talent in the 32-nation championship.

Competition for spots in the final squad is intensifying following the announcement of the extended training squad.

Selectors are expected to confirm the final 16-player team on 22nd June.

Fiji faces a tough challenge in the group stage after being drawn alongside powerhouse nations Croatia, France and Egypt in Pool A.

The tournament will be held from 29th July to 9th August and will also feature defending champions Spain.

While the draw presents a formidable test, simply reaching the World Championship is a remarkable achievement for the sport in Fiji and a testament to the team's hard work and determination.

Fiji and Mexico are the only nations making their debut at this year's tournament, adding further significance to Fiji's historic journey onto the world stage.

item.title: Against the odds: Swami Vivekananda College swims to gold and silver glory
item.description: Overcoming the challenges of managing more than 50 swimmers with only two teachers and travelling from Nadi to Suva for competition, Swami Vivekananda College emerged as one of the top-performing schools at the National Age Group Swimming Championships. Years of sacrifice and hard work ended in joy and relief for the school after the team claimed gold in the boys' division and silver in the girls'
item.content:encoded:
Overcoming the challenges of managing more than 50 swimmers with only two teachers and travelling from Nadi to Suva for competition, Swami Vivekananda College emerged as one of the top-performing schools at the National Age Group Swimming Championships.

Years of sacrifice and hard work ended in joy and relief for the school after the team claimed gold in the boys' division and silver in the girls' division.

Team manager Vika Bakewa says the result was deeply emotional, reflecting years of preparation, setbacks and determination that were finally rewarded on the national stage.

Bakewa says the victory was even more special as the boys had not won the title since 2022, while the girls' silver medal ended a long wait for a podium finish after years of dominance by rival schools.

She says the journey had been far from easy, with the biggest challenge being the cost and logistics of travelling from Nadi to Suva for competition.

However, she praised the school administration and principal for their unwavering support, including providing transport and assisting the team throughout their campaign, adding that without that backing, the team's participation and success would not have been possible.

Bakewa also acknowledged the strong role played by parents, saying their financial contributions and constant encouragement helped keep the swimmers focused throughout the championships.

She says parents were present daily during the championships, checking in on their children and supporting them in every way possible.

She adds that old scholars and members of the wider community also stepped in to assist, particularly given the challenge of having only two teachers manage a squad of more than 50 swimmers.

The team manager also highlighted that swimming at the school is a continuous process, with athletes already preparing for upcoming championships and working to defend and improve their performances next season.

She says the success has motivated the squad to push even harder, adding that the experience has strengthened both the swimmers and the support system behind them.

She adds that while celebrations will follow upon their return to Nadi, the focus remains on growth and maintaining standards going forward.

item.title: Lack of 50m pool in the west no barrier as Drasa Avenue retain girls' title
item.description: Training without access to a 50-metre pool in the Western Division did not stop Drasa Avenue School from retaining the girls' division title at the National Schools Age Group Swimming Championships. Drasa Avenue coach and Fiji National Primary Schools Swimming Association president Salaseni Lele says the team had to adapt its preparation significantly ahead of the long-course competition, with swi
item.content:encoded:
Training without access to a 50-metre pool in the Western Division did not stop Drasa Avenue School from retaining the girls' division title at the National Schools Age Group Swimming Championships.

Drasa Avenue coach and Fiji National Primary Schools Swimming Association president Salaseni Lele says the team had to adapt its preparation significantly ahead of the long-course competition, with swimmers building endurance and refining technique despite limited facilities.

Lele says most of the squad were development swimmers who were prepared within a short period of just one to two months, making the title defence even more satisfying.

She says despite the challenges, the Drasa girls delivered strong performances across all age groups, including setting a national record in the 11–12 years 4x50m medley relay.

She adds that the lack of a 50-metre pool in the West remains a major hurdle in preparation, but the swimmers showed resilience and determination to defend their title.

Lele praised the support of assistant coaches, parents and the school community in Lautoka, saying their contribution was vital in ensuring the team was ready to compete at a high level.

She says the girls held off strong competition from other schools with longer preparation periods and better facilities, proving their consistency once again on the national stage.

Looking ahead, Lele says the focus now shifts to upcoming championships, with continued athlete development and pathways into club swimming.

item.title: PSG beat Arsenal on penalties to retain Champions League title
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain are Champions League winners once again after beating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest

item.title: Ball retention will be key - Kolinisau
item.description: Ball retention will be key as the Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team prepares for its final pool match and looks ahead to the Cup quarter-final at the World Championship in Valladolid, Spain. Kolinisau says numbers at the breakdown will be crucial as well, and making sure they have the right numbers at the breakdown is important.
item.content:encoded:
Ball retention will be key as the Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team prepares for its final pool match and looks ahead to the Cup quarter-final at the World Championship in Valladolid, Spain.

Kolinisau says numbers at the breakdown will be crucial as well, and making sure they have the right numbers at the breakdown is important.

He says players should not doubt themselves when executing decisions on the field, and having the right mindset will be vital as teams are competing on the mental aspect of the game.

Kolinisau reminded the players that discipline will be key, and the players should maintain their composure during the knockout stages.

Fiji Airways is through to the Cup quarterfinals and will next play hosts Spain in their last pool match at 12.12am tomorrow.

The Cup quarterfinals are at 2.27am tomorrow.

The Cup semi finals are at 8.44pm tomorrow before the Cup final kicks off at 2.26am Monday.

Meanwhile, the Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side suffered a 26-19 defeat to the USA before being thrashed 45-5 by Australia in their opening two pool matches.

The Women's cup quarter finals are at 12.49am tomorrow.

The cup semifinals are at 8pm tomorrow, while the Women's cup final kicks off at 1.46am Monday.

item.title: Kolinisau calls for consistency ahead of Australia semi-final
item.description: The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team will need to maintain the flair, discipline and consistency displayed in their quarter-final victory over New Zealand as they prepare to face Australia in the semi-finals of the Valladolid Championship in Spain. Head coach Osea Kolinisau says improving numbers at the breakdown was a key focus area heading into the quarter-final, and the players delivered on
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team will need to maintain the flair, discipline and consistency displayed in their quarter-final victory over New Zealand as they prepare to face Australia in the semi-finals of the Valladolid Championship in Spain.

Head coach Osea Kolinisau says improving numbers at the breakdown was a key focus area heading into the quarter-final, and the players delivered on that plan with an emphatic 40-0 win over New Zealand.

He stressed that discipline remains crucial at this stage of the competition and praised the players for maintaining their composure and structure throughout the quarter-final encounter.

Fiji will take on Australia in the first semi-final at 8.44pm, with a place in the Cup final at stake.

Argentina will face South Africa in the second semi-final at 9.06pm.

The Cup final is scheduled for 2.26am tomorrow.

Meanwhile, the Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side will face Spain in the seventh-place play-off at 11.04pm.

item.title: Moana upset Brumbies 21-19 in final round to end season
item.description: An undermanned Moana Pasifika have capped off their potential final match with a stirring victory, upsetting the finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19. However, there were mixed emotions as players celebrated a rare win before joining staff to mark the occasion of the club's farewell game and expected demise with an emotionally charged hymn.
item.content:encoded:
An undermanned Moana Pasifika have capped off their potential final match with a stirring victory, upsetting the finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19.

However, there were mixed emotions as players celebrated a rare win before joining staff to mark the occasion of the club's farewell game and expected demise with an emotionally charged hymn.

The Super Rugby Pacific club was placed into liquidation on Monday after its current owners announced on April 15th that they would not continue to fund the franchise beyond 2026.

Unless a saviour emerges to rescue the club, which was only established in 2022, it will fold, and the competition will shrink to 10 teams in 2027.

Moana relished the occasion at GIO Stadium last night and snapped a 12-game losing streak to avoid a record-equalling 13th loss.

In other results, the Western Force defeated the Waratahs 31-25, while the Chiefs thrashed the Blues 59-34.

item.title: Reapi Ulunisau becomes first Fijiana centurion with 100th try
item.description: Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s star Reapi Ulunisau has etched her name into the history books after becoming the first Fijian player to score 100 tries for the national women's sevens team. Ulunisau reached the remarkable milestone during Fiji's final pool match against South Africa at the Valladolid World Rugby Sevens Championship in Spain.
item.content:encoded:
Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s star Reapi Ulunisau has etched her name into the history books after becoming the first Fijian player to score 100 tries for the national women's sevens team.

Ulunisau reached the remarkable milestone during Fiji's final pool match against South Africa at the Valladolid World Rugby Sevens Championship in Spain.

Her 100th try came in spectacular fashion, with the 32-year-old winger and utility back sprinting more than 70 metres to score and help Fiji secure a hard-fought 17-12 victory.

A veteran of both rugby 7's and 15's, Ulunisau has enjoyed an illustrious international career.

She was part of Fiji's medal-winning campaigns at the Tokyo 2020 Olympic Games and the 2022 Commonwealth Games in Birmingham, and also represented the country at the Paris Olympics.

Despite the historic achievement, the Fijiana team's campaign suffered a setback in the quarter-finals of the Valladolid Sevens, where they were defeated 40-14 by the United States.

Fijiana will now take on Spain in the seventh-place play-off, scheduled for 11.04pm today.

item.title: Valladolid 7s: Fijiana 7s beat South Africa 17-12
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 30/05/2026 The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side defeated South Africa 17-12 in their last match of the World Championship Valladolid 7s tournament tonight.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 30/05/2026

The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side defeated South Africa 17-12 in their last match of the World Championship Valladolid 7s tournament tonight.

Atelaite Buna Ralivanawa, Rogosau Adimereani and Reapi Ulunisau scored a try each to help the Fijiana 7s side to book a place in the playoffs.

South Africa try came from Byrhandre Dolf and Maria Tshiremba.

item.title: Maharaj shines with multiple gold medals at Swimming Championship
item.description: 11-year-old Leila Maharaj is delivering a commanding performance at the National Schools Age Group Swimming Championships, current

item.title: Messi to represent Argentina at sixth World Cup
item.description: Lionel Messi will play in a sixth FIFA World Cup as he captains defending champions Argentina at the 2026 tournament.The forward,

item.title: SVC leads boys, Drasa tops girls at National Schools Age Group Swimming Championships
item.description: Swami Vivekananda College is putting on a dominant display at the National Schools Age Group Swimming Championships, topping the b

item.title: French Open: Fonseca stuns Djokovic in third-round epic
item.description: Novak Djokovic's wait for a record 25th Grand Slam singles title goes on after teenage star Joao Fonseca produced a stunning fight
